tasks.label: Arrange contents insurance from day one of the occupation contract
Terminate or move (if possible) your existing broadband account
Book broadband install for move-in day or shortly thereafter
tasks.dueText: 1+ month ahead, but may be blocked by an existing active account

tasks.label: Arrange moving-van access and a parking permit (e.g. with the council if on-street, or with building management if available)
Set up Royal Mail post redirection
Update your TV Licence
Collect all keys and fobs: front door, back door, windows, mailbox, communal, garage/parking, bin store, cycle store, alarm codes
Test every key/fob in every lock
Test the door entry / intercom / buzzer system and any communal-door fobs
Take date-stamped photos of gas, electricity, water and heating meters
tasks.dueText: Ensure the meter serial number is visible

tasks.label: Check for any outstanding debt left by prior contract-holders if any meter is prepayment (key/card/PAYG)
Take a video walkthrough of every room, narrating and zooming in on any defects
Photograph inside the oven, fridge and freezer (including all drawers), washing machine drum and seal, and dishwasher filter
Photograph inside every cupboard, wardrobe and drawer across all rooms
Photograph any damage to floors, walls, skirting, ceilings, doors, windows and garden (scuffs, marks, scratches, stains, mould, chipped tiles, burns or empty spots)
Photograph all curtains, blinds and lampshades (fixed and portable)
Check the inventory line by line against actual item condition -- note where condition is worse than stated, or where photos are not high enough resolution to show damage
Test all smoke alarms (one is required on every storey) and the carbon monoxide alarm (required in any room with a gas, oil or solid-fuel appliance) under the Fitness for Human Habitation rules
Locate the water stopcock, gas shut-off valve and consumer unit / fuse board
tasks.dueText: Ask the landlord or letting agents if not obvious

tasks.label: Test the boiler, hot water, heating and every individual radiator
Run every tap (including outdoor taps and showers/baths) and flush every toilet
Test every hob ring, oven, extractor fan, microwave, kettle and other provided kitchen appliances
Test all bulbs, including within the extractor fan and any workspace / countertop lighting
Check every window opens, closes and locks
Check for damp, mould and pest evidence (droppings or smell behind furniture / major appliances)
Confirm the landlord/letting agent emergency and out-of-hours repair contact details (check your occupation contract)
Submit your own inventory amendment promptly with all comments and photos by email
Open or move accounts and submit meter readings to gas, electricity and heating suppliers
Set up your water and sewerage account with Dŵr Cymru Welsh Water (or Hafren Dyfrdwy in parts of north-east / mid Wales)
Find out refuse and recycling collection days and bin/recycling store arrangements
Register for council tax with your new local authority
Apply for single person discount (25%) if living alone
Apply for council tax student exemption or disregard if applicable
Apply for disabled band reduction if applicable
Apply for Council Tax Reduction if on a low income
Update Child Benefit address if applicable
Update Universal Credit address if applicable
Update State Pension address if applicable
Register with a local NHS Wales GP surgery
Register for NHS dental care in Wales via the Dental Access Portal
Update your nominated pharmacy with your new address for repeat prescription delivery
tasks.dueText: Can be done via your GP or the pharmacy directly

tasks.label: Set up a standing order for rent
Update all banks, building societies, mortgage lenders and credit cards with your new address
Update ISA, investment, exchange and brokerage accounts with your new address
Update life insurance, critical illness and income protection with your new address
Update solicitor, accountant and will/executor records with your new address
Update your DVLA driving licence address (free; up to £1,000 fine if not done)
Update your DVLA V5C vehicle log book
Update your vehicle insurance address
Update your Blue Badge permit address
Update workplace and personal pension providers with your new address
Notify HMRC of your address change (Income Tax, National Insurance, tax credits)
Update Companies House registered office and director's service address if running a business
Update the Disclosure and Barring Service (DBS) Update Service
Update Student Finance Wales address if a student
Update vet records and microchip database (Petlog/Identibase) if you have pets
Register on the electoral roll (for Senedd and local elections)
Update Gift Aid declarations with charities with your new address
Update PayPal, Amazon, eBay and other frequently used online retailer default shipping addresses
Update gym and other subscription services (e.g. mobile provider) with your new address
Receive your written statement of the occupation contract
tasks.dueText: Landlord must provide within 14 days of occupation (Renting Homes (Wales) Act 2016)

tasks.label: Check your landlord is registered and your landlord or agent is licensed using the Rent Smart Wales public register
Check you were not charged any banned letting fees and that any holding deposit did not exceed 1 week's rent (Renting Homes (Fees etc.) (Wales) Act 2019)
Receive Deposit Protection prescribed information (confirm the deposit is protected in DPS, mydeposits or TDS)
tasks.dueText: Landlord must protect within 30 days. Note: Wales has no statutory cap on the security deposit amount.

tasks.label: Receive the Energy Performance Certificate (EPC)
Confirm a valid Electrical Installation Condition Report (EICR) is in place (required at least every 5 years under the Fitness for Human Habitation rules)
Receive the Gas Safety Certificate, if gas is connected
Check whether an HMO licence is required and held, and that the property is licensed via Rent Smart Wales / your local authority
